I’m reading the relevant method bodies now so the final map can say what each dependent relies on, not just that it mentions `Story`.

    if (privileged = tags.select { |t| !t.can_be_applied_by?(u) }).any?
      raise "#{u.username} does not have permission to use privileged tag(s): #{privileged.map(&:tag).join(" ")}"

  def comments_anchor
    "comments_#{token}"
  end

  def description=(desc)
    self[:description] = desc.to_s.rstrip
    self.markeddown_description = generated_markeddown_description
  end

  def description_or_story_text(chars = 0)
    s = if description.present?
      markeddown_description.gsub(/<[^>]*>/, "")
    else
      story_text&.body
    end

    if chars > 0 && s.to_s.length > chars
      # remove last truncated word
      s = s.to_s[0, chars].gsub(/ [^ ]*\z/, "")
    end

    HtmlEncoder.decode(s.to_s)
  end

  def domain_search_url
    "/search?order=newest&q=domain:#{domain}"
  end

  def fix_bogus_chars
    # this is needlessly complicated to work around character encoding issues
    # that arise when doing just self.title.to_s.gsub(160.chr, "")
    self.title = title.to_s.chars.map { |chr|
      if chr.ord == 160
        " "
      else
        chr
      end
    }.join("")

    true
  end

  def generated_markeddown_description
    Markdowner.to_html(description, allow_images: can_have_images?, as_of: created_at)
  end

  # TODO: race condition: if two votes arrive at the same time, the second one
  # won't take the first's score change into effect for calculated_hotness
  def update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)
    self.score += score_delta
    self.flags += flag_delta
    Story.connection.execute <<~SQL
      UPDATE stories SET
        score = (
          select sum(vote) from votes where story_id = stories.id and comment_id is null and (
            (vote = 1) or
            (
              vote = -1 and
              not exists ( -- user didn't comment
                select 1 from comments where votes.user_id = comments.user_id and comments.story_id = stories.id
              )
            )
          )
        ),
        flags = (select count(*) from votes where story_id = stories.id and comment_id is null and
          vote = -1 and
          not exists ( -- user didn't comment
            select 1 from comments where votes.user_id = comments.user_id and comments.story_id = stories.id
          )
        ),
        hotness = #{calculated_hotness}
      WHERE id = #{id.to_i}
    SQL
  end

  def has_suggestions?
    suggested_taggings.any? || suggested_titles.any?
  end

  def header_anchor
    "header_#{token}"
  end

  # calling .count on a preloaded association still triggers a count query
  def hider_count
    @hider_count ||= hidings.to_a.size
  end

  def disownable_by_user?(user)
    !new_record? && user&.id == user_id && created_at < DELETEABLE_DAYS.days.ago
  end

  def is_flaggable?(user)
    return false if user&.id == user_id
    if created_at && self.score > FLAGGABLE_MIN_SCORE
      Time.current - created_at <= FLAGGABLE_DAYS.days
    else
      false
    end
  end

  def is_editable_by_user?(user)
    return false if user.nil? || user.new_record? || # assumption: cabinet view
      new_record? # assumption: previewing

    if user&.id == user_id
      if is_moderated?
        false
      else
        created_at.after?(MAX_EDIT_MINS.minutes.ago)
      end
    else
      false
    end
  end

  def is_gone?
    is_deleted? || (user.is_banned? && score < 0)
  end

  def is_hidden_by_user?(user)
    !!HiddenStory.find_by(user_id: user.id, story_id: id)
  end

  def is_recent?
    created_at >= RECENT_DAYS.days.ago
  end

  def is_saved_by_user?(user)
    !!SavedStory.find_by(user_id: user.id, story_id: id)
  end

  def is_unavailable
    !unavailable_at.nil?
  end

  def is_unavailable=(what)
    self.unavailable_at = ((what.to_i == 1 && !is_unavailable) ? Time.current : nil)
  end

  def is_undeletable_by_user?(user)
    if user&.is_moderator?
      true
    elsif user && user.id == user_id && !is_moderated?
      true
    else
      false
    end
  end

  def log_moderation
    if new_record? || (!editing_from_suggestions && (!editor || editor.id == user_id))
      return
    end

    all_changes = changes.merge(tag_changes)
    all_changes.delete("normalized_url")
    all_changes.delete("unavailable_at")
    all_changes.delete("last_edited_at")

    if !all_changes.any?
      return
    end

    m = Moderation.new
    if editing_from_suggestions
      m.is_from_suggestions = true
    else
      m.moderator_user_id = editor.try(:id)
    end
    m.story_id = id

    m.action = all_changes.map { |k, v|
      if k == "is_deleted" && is_deleted?
        "deleted story"
      elsif k == "is_deleted" && !is_deleted?
        "undeleted story"
      elsif k == "merged_story_id"
        if v[1]
          "merged into #{merged_into_story.short_id} " \
            "(#{merged_into_story.title})"
        else
          "unmerged from another story"
        end
      elsif v[0].is_a?(String) && v[1].is_a?(String) && v[0].present? && v[1].blank?
        "blanked #{k} from #{v[0].inspect}"
      else
        "changed #{k} from #{v[0].inspect} to #{v[1].inspect}"
      end
    }.join(", ")

    m.reason = moderation_reason
    m.save!

    self.is_moderated = true
  end

  def mailing_list_message_id
    "story.#{short_id}.#{created_at.to_i}@#{Rails.application.domain}"
  end

  def mark_submitter
    Keystore.increment_value_for("user:#{user_id}:stories_submitted")
  end

  # unordered, use Comment.thread_sorted_comments for presenting threads
  def merged_comments
    return Comment.none unless id # unsaved Stories have no comments

    Comment.joins(:story)
      .where(story: {merged_story_id: id})
      .or(Comment.where(story_id: id))
  end

  def merge_story_short_id=(sid)
    self.merged_story_id = sid.present? ? Story.where(short_id: sid).pick(:id) : nil
  end

  def merge_story_short_id
    merged_story_id ? merged_into_story.try(:short_id) : nil
  end

  def record_initial_upvote
    Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(1, id, nil, user_id, nil, false)
  end

  def show_score_to_user?(u)
    u&.is_moderator? || !current_flagged?
  end

  def tag_changes
    # 'tags_was' is bad grammar but is named to mirror ActiveModel::Dirty's convention of providing
    # a 'attribute_was' reader. The AR associations API is pretty broad, so rather than try to
    # override all the methods to maintain state, this exception should prompt you to only use
    # the tags= method. See StoriesController#update for an example.
    raise "Controller didn't save tags_was for edit logging" if tags_was.nil?

    old_tag_names = tags_was.map(&:tag).join(" ")
    new_tag_names = tags.map(&:tag).join(" ")

    if old_tag_names == new_tag_names
      {}
    else
      {"tags" => [old_tag_names, new_tag_names]}
    end
  end

  def save_suggested_tags_for_user!(new_tag_names_a, user)
    suggested_taggings.where(user_id: user.id).delete_all

    new_suggested_tags = Tag
      .active
      .where(tag: new_tag_names_a.uniq.compact_blank)
      .map { |t| {user: user, story: self, tag: t} }
    SuggestedTagging.create!(new_suggested_tags)

    # if enough users voted on the same set of replacement tags, do it
    tag_votes = {}
    suggested_taggings.group_by(&:user_id).each do |_u, stg|
      stg.each do |s|
        tag_votes[s.tag.tag] ||= 0
        tag_votes[s.tag.tag] += 1
      end
    end

    final_tags = []
    tag_votes.each do |k, v|
      if v >= SUGGESTION_QUORUM
        final_tags.push k
      end
    end

    if final_tags.any? && (final_tags.sort != tags.map(&:tag).sort)
      # Rails.logger.info "[s#{id}] promoting suggested tags " \
      #  "#{final_tags.inspect} instead of #{tags.map(&:tag).inspect}"
      self.editor = nil
      self.editing_from_suggestions = true
      self.moderation_reason = "Automatically changed from user suggestions"
      self.tags_was = tags.to_a
      Story.transaction do
        self.tags = Tag.where(tag: final_tags)
        save!
      end
    end
  end

  def save_suggested_title_for_user!(title, user)
    st = suggested_titles.find_by(user_id: user.id)
    if !st
      st = suggested_titles.build
      st.user_id = user.id
    end
    st.title = title
    st.save!

    # if enough users voted on the same exact title, save it
    title_votes = {}
    suggested_titles.each do |s|
      title_votes[s.title] ||= 0
      title_votes[s.title] += 1
    end

    title_votes.sort_by { |_k, v| v }.reverse_each do |kv|
      if kv[1] >= SUGGESTION_QUORUM
        # Rails.logger.info "[s#{id}] promoting suggested title " \
        #   "#{kv[0].inspect} instead of #{self.title.inspect}"
        self.editor = nil
        self.editing_from_suggestions = true
        self.moderation_reason = "Automatically changed from user suggestions"
        self.title = kv[0]
        self.tags_was = tags.to_a
        if !save
          # Rails.logger.error "[s#{id}] failed auto promoting: " << errors.inspect
        end

        break
      end
    end
  end

  INVISIBLE_CHARACTERS = Regexp.union(
    # Sourced from https://regex101.com/r/42jDca/1
    /\u0000-\u001F/, # control characters
    /\uFFF0-\uFFF8/, # interlinear annotation
    /\u007F/, # DELETE
    # and from https://invisible-characters.com/
    /\u3164/, # HANGUL FILLER
    /\uFFA0/, # HALFWIDTH HANGUL FILLER
    /\uFFFC/, # OBJECT REPLACEMENT CHARACTER
    /\u00A0/, # NO-BREAK SPACE
    /\u00AD/, # SOFT HYPHEN
    /\u034F/, # COMBINING GRAPHEME JOINER
    /\u061C/, # ARABIC LETTER MARK
    /\u115F/, # HANGUL CHOSEONG FILLER
    /\u1160/, # HANGUL JUNGSEONG FILLER
    /\u1680/, # OGHAM SPACE MARK

# typed: false

class Story < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :user
  belongs_to :domain, optional: true, counter_cache: true
  belongs_to :origin, optional: true, counter_cache: true
  belongs_to :merged_into_story,
    class_name: "Story",
    counter_cache: :stories_count,
    foreign_key: "merged_story_id",
    inverse_of: :merged_stories,
    optional: true
  has_many :merged_stories,
    class_name: "Story",
    foreign_key: "merged_story_id",
    inverse_of: :merged_into_story,
    dependent: :nullify
  has_many :taggings,
    autosave: true,
    dependent: :destroy # tests fail if :restrict_with_exception
  has_many :suggested_taggings, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
  has_many :suggested_tags, source: :story, through: :suggested_taggings, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
  has_many :suggested_titles, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
  has_many :suggested_tagging_times,
    -> { group(:tag_id).select("count(*) as times, tag_id").order(times: :desc) },
    class_name: "SuggestedTagging",
    inverse_of: :story
  has_many :suggested_title_times,
    -> { group(:title).select("count(*) as times, title").order(times: :desc) },
    class_name: "SuggestedTitle",
    inverse_of: :story
  has_many :comments,
    inverse_of: :story,
    dependent: :restrict_with_exception
  has_many :tags, -> { order("tags.is_media desc, tags.tag") }, through: :taggings
  has_many :votes, -> { where(comment_id: nil) },
    inverse_of: :story,
    dependent: :destroy # tests fail if :restrict_with_exception
  has_many :voters, -> { where("votes.comment_id" => nil) },
    through: :votes,
    source: :user
  has_many :hidings, class_name: "HiddenStory", inverse_of: :story, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
  has_many :savings, class_name: "SavedStory", inverse_of: :story, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
  has_one :story_text, foreign_key: :id, dependent: :restrict_with_exception, inverse_of: :story
  has_many :links,
    inverse_of: :from_story,
    dependent: :destroy # tests fail if :restrict_with_exception
  has_many :incoming_links,
    class_name: "Link",
    inverse_of: :to_story,
    dependent: :restrict_with_exception
  has_one :moderation, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
  has_many :read_ribbons, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
  has_many :saved_stories, dependent: :restrict_with_exception

  validates :short_id,
    presence: true,
    uniqueness: {case_sensitive: false},
    length: {maximum: 6}

  scope :base, ->(user, unmerged: true) {
    q = includes(:hidings, :story_text, :user).not_deleted(user).mod_preload?(user)
    q = q.unmerged if unmerged
    q
  }
  scope :for_presentation, -> {
    includes(:domain, :origin, :hidings, :user, :tags, taggings: :tag)
  }
  scope :mod_preload?, ->(user) {
    user.try(:is_moderator?) ? preload(:suggested_taggings, :suggested_titles) : all
  }
  scope :mod_single_preload?, ->(user) {
    user.try(:is_moderator?) ? preload(:suggested_taggings, :suggested_titles, hidings: :user, votes: :user) : all
  }
  scope :deleted, -> { where(is_deleted: true) }
  scope :not_deleted, ->(user) {
    user.try(:is_moderator?) ? all : where(is_deleted: false).or(where(user_id: user.try(:id).to_i))
  }
  scope :unmerged, -> { where(merged_story_id: nil) }
  scope :positive_ranked, -> { where("score >= 0") }
  scope :low_scoring, ->(max = 5) { where("score < ?", max) }
  scope :front_page, -> { hottest.limit(StoriesPaginator::STORIES_PER_PAGE) }
  scope :hottest, ->(user = nil, exclude_tags = nil) {
    base(user).not_hidden_by(user)
      .filter_tags(exclude_tags || [])
      .positive_ranked
      .order(:hotness)
  }

  scope :tagged, ->(user, tags) {
    base(user)
      .positive_ranked
      .where(
        Tagging
          .where("story_id = stories.id")
          .where(tag_id: tags)
          .arel.exists
      )
      .order(created_at: :desc)
  }

  scope :top, ->(user, length, exclude_tags = nil) {
    raise ArgumentError, "Invalid interval" unless IntervalHelper::TIME_INTERVALS.value?(length[:intv].capitalize)

    top = base(user)
      .where("created_at >= (NOW() - INTERVAL ? #{length[:intv].upcase})", length[:dur])
      .filter_tags(exclude_tags || [])
    top.order(score: :desc)
  }

  scope :categories, ->(user, categories) {
    base(user)
      .positive_ranked
      .where(
        Tagging
          .joins(:tag)
          .where("story_id = stories.id")
          .where(tag: {category_id: categories})
          .arel.exists
      )
      .order(created_at: :desc)
  }

  scope :recent, ->(user = nil, exclude_tags = nil, unmerged: true) {
    base(user, unmerged: unmerged).not_hidden_by(user)
      .filter_tags(exclude_tags || [])
      .low_scoring
      .where("created_at >= ?", 10.days.ago)
      .where.not(id: front_page.ids)
      .order("stories.created_at DESC")
  }
  scope :filter_tags, ->(tags) {
    tags.empty? ? all : where(
      Story.arel_table[:id].not_in(
        Tagging.where(tag_id: tags).select(:story_id).arel
      )
    )
  }
  scope :filter_tags_for, ->(user) {
    user.nil? ? all : where(
      Story.arel_table[:id].not_in(
        Tagging.joins(tag: :tag_filters)
          .where(tag_filters: {user_id: user})
          .select(:story_id).arel
      )
    )
  }
  scope :hidden_by, ->(user) {
    user.nil? ? none : joins(:hidings).merge(HiddenStory.by(user))
  }
  scope :not_hidden_by, ->(user) {
    user.nil? ? all : where.not(
      HiddenStory.select("TRUE")
        .where(Arel.sql("hidden_stories.story_id = stories.id"))
        .by(user)
        .arel
        .exists
    )
  }
  scope :saved_by, ->(user) {
    user.nil? ? none : joins(:savings).merge(SavedStory.by(user))
  }
  scope :to_mastodon, -> {
    hottest(nil, Tag.where(tag: "meta").ids)
      .where(mastodon_id: nil)
      .where("score >= 2")
      .where("created_at >= ?", 2.days.ago)
      .limit(10)
  }

  scope :hidden, ->(user, exclude_tags = nil) {
    base(user)
      .hidden_by(user)
      .filter_tags(exclude_tags || [])
      .order(:hotness)
  }

  scope :newest, ->(user, exclude_tags = nil) {
    base(user, unmerged: false)
      .filter_tags(exclude_tags || [])
      .order(id: :desc)
  }

  scope :active, ->(user, exclude_tags = []) {
    base(user)
      .where.not(id: hidden_by(user).select(:id))
      .filter_tags(exclude_tags)
      .order(last_comment_at: :desc)
  }

  scope :saved, ->(user, exclude_tags = []) {
    base(user)
      .saved_by(user)
      .filter_tags(exclude_tags)
      .order(:hotness)
  }

  scope :newest_by_user, ->(user, submitter) {
    where(user: submitter)
      .includes(:tags)
      .not_deleted(user)
      .mod_preload?(user)
      .order(id: :desc)
  }

  include Token

  TITLE_MIN_LENGTH = 3
  TITLE_MAX_LENGTH = 150

  validates :title, length: {in: TITLE_MIN_LENGTH..TITLE_MAX_LENGTH}, presence: true
  validates :description, length: {maximum: 65_535}
  validates :url, length: {maximum: 250, allow_nil: true}
  validates :short_id, presence: true, length: {maximum: 6}
  validates :markeddown_description, length: {maximum: 16_777_215, allow_nil: true}
  validates :mastodon_id, length: {maximum: 25, allow_nil: true}
  validates :twitter_id, length: {maximum: 20, allow_nil: true}
  validates :is_deleted, :is_moderated, :user_is_author, :user_is_following, inclusion: {in: [true, false]}
  validates :score, :flags, :hotness, :comments_count, presence: true
  validates :normalized_url, length: {maximum: 255, allow_nil: true}
  validates :last_edited_at, presence: true

  validates_each :merged_story_id do |record, _attr, value|
    if value.to_i == record.id
      record.errors.add(:merge_story_short_id, "id cannot be itself.")
    end
  end

  COMMENTABLE_DAYS = 90
  FLAGGABLE_DAYS = 14
  DELETEABLE_DAYS = FLAGGABLE_DAYS * 2

  # the lowest a score can go
  FLAGGABLE_MIN_SCORE = -5

  # after this many minutes old, a story cannot be edited
  MAX_EDIT_MINS = (60 * 6)

  # days a story is considered recent, for resubmitting
  RECENT_DAYS = 30

  # users needed to make similar suggestions go live
  SUGGESTION_QUORUM = 2

  # let a hot story linger for this many seconds
  HOTNESS_WINDOW = 60 * 60 * 22

  # drop these words from titles when making URLs
  TITLE_DROP_WORDS = ["", "a", "an", "and", "but", "in", "of", "or", "that", "the", "to"].freeze

  # Dingbats, emoji, and other graphics https://www.unicode.org/charts/
  GRAPHICS_RE = /[\u{0000}-\u{001F}\u{2190}\u{2192}-\u{27BF}\u{1F000}-\u{1F9FF}]/

  attr_accessor :current_vote, :editing_from_suggestions, :editor, :fetching_ip,
    :is_hidden_by_cur_user, :latest_comment_id, :is_saved_by_cur_user, :moderation_reason,
    :new_user_acculturation_error, :previewing, :tags_was
  attr_writer :fetched_response

  before_validation :assign_initial_attributes, on: :create
  before_save :log_moderation

before_save :log_moderation
  before_save :fix_bogus_chars
  after_create :mark_submitter, :record_initial_upvote
  after_save :bust_comment_redirect_cache, :recreate_links, :update_cached_columns, :update_story_text

  validate do
    if url.present?
      check_already_posted_recently?
      check_not_banned_domain
      check_not_banned_origin
      check_not_new_domain_from_new_user
      check_not_brigading
      check_not_pushcx_stream
      errors.add(:url, "is not valid") unless url.match(Utils::URL_RE)
    elsif description.to_s.strip == ""
      errors.add(:description, "must contain text if no URL posted")
    end

    if title.starts_with?("Ask") && tags.map(&:tag).include?("ask")
      errors.add(:title, " starting 'Ask #{Rails.application.name}' or similar is redundant " \
                          "with the ask tag.")
    end
    if title.match(GRAPHICS_RE)
      errors.add(:title, " may not contain emoji, dingbats, or other graphics")
    end
    if !title.empty? && title.ascii_only? && title == title.upcase && !(title =~ /WWDC .*\d{4}/ && (user&.username == "calvin" || user&.is_moderator?))
      errors.add(:title, " doesn't need to scream, ASCII has supported lowercase since June 17, 1963.")
    end

    if !errors.any? && url.blank?
      self.user_is_author = true
    end

    check_tags
  end

  def self./(short_id)
    find_by! short_id:
  end

  def accepting_comments?
    !is_gone? &&
      !previewing &&
      (new_record? || created_at.after?(COMMENTABLE_DAYS.days.ago))
  end

  def already_posted_recently?
    return false unless url.present? && new_record?

    if most_recent_similar&.is_recent?
      true
    elsif user&.is_new? && most_recent_similar
      true
    else
      false
    end
  end

  # on merge, bust the cache for redirecting /c/abc123 -> /s/def456/title#c_abc123
  # see CommentsController#redirect_from_short_id
  def bust_comment_redirect_cache
    return unless merged_story_id_previously_changed?

    Rails.cache.delete_multi comments.pluck(:short_id).map { "c_#{it}" }
  end

  def check_already_posted_recently?
    return unless url.present? && new_record?

    if most_recent_similar&.is_recent?
      errors.add(:url, "has already been submitted within the past #{RECENT_DAYS} days")
    elsif user&.is_new? && most_recent_similar
      errors.add(:url, "cannot be resubmitted by new users")
    end
  end

  def check_not_new_domain_from_new_user
    return unless url.present? && new_record? && domain

    if user&.is_new? && domain.stories.not_deleted(nil).count == 0
      errors.add :url, "is an unseen domain from a new user."
      self.new_user_acculturation_error = ModMail.create_inviter_discussion! user: user, message: <<~MESSAGE
        Hi,

        #{user.username}: This has a high false-positive rate, but we don't allow new users to submit links to domains we haven't seen before.
        This is to give you a time to learn about topicality and to discourage immediate self-promotion.

        #{user.invited_by_user&.username}: This is a good opportunity for you to introduce #{user.username} to the [site guideline](https://lobste.rs/about#guidelines) around topicality and self-promo.

        If you need, you can talk to the mods in this conversation, but we'll probably stay out of it unless asked.

        #{submission_markdown}
      MESSAGE
    end
  end

  def check_not_banned_domain
    return unless url.present? && new_record? && domain

    if domain.banned?
      ModNote.tattle_on_story_domain!(self, "banned")
      errors.add(:url, "is from banned domain #{domain.domain}: #{domain.banned_reason}")
    end
  end

  def check_not_banned_origin
    return unless url.present? && new_record? && origin

    if origin.banned?
      ModNote.tattle_on_story_origin!(self, "banned")
      errors.add(:url, "is from banned origin #{origin.identifier}: #{origin.banned_reason}")
    end
  end

  def check_not_brigading
    return if url.blank? || !new_record? || !(
      url.match?(%r{^https?://bitbucket.org/[^/]+/[^/]+/(issues|pull-requests)/}) ||
      url.match?(%r{^https?://bugs.launchpad.net/[^/]+/\+bug/}) ||
      url.match?(%r{^https?://chiselapp.com/user/[^/]+/repository/[^/]+/tktview/}) ||
      url.match?(%r{^https?://codeberg.org/[^/]+/[^/]+/(issues|pulls)/}) ||
      url.match?(%r{^https?://github.com/[^/]+/[^/]+/(discussions|issues|pull)/}) ||
      url.match?(%r{^https?://gitlab.com/.+/(issues|merge_requests)/}) ||
      url.match?(%r{^https?://savannah.gnu.org/bugs/}) ||
      url.match?(%r{^https?://sourceforge.net/p/[^/]+/(support|tickets)/})
    )

    if user.is_new?
      errors.add :url, "is a project's bug tracker or discussions"
      self.new_user_acculturation_error = ModMail.create_inviter_discussion! user: user, message: <<~MESSAGE
        Hi,

        #{user.username}: We don't accept links into projects' bug trackers or discussions to avoid brigading our readers into their community spaces,
        and because Lobsters doesn't have good threads when we're dropped without context into the middle of a controversy.

        #{user.invited_by_user&.username}: This is a good opportunity for you to introduce #{user.username} to the [brigading guideline](https://lobste.rs/about#brigading) and maybe help them find a better link, like an overview from a neutral third party.

        If you need, you can talk to the mods in this conversation, but we'll probably stay out of it unless asked.

        #{submission_markdown}
      MESSAGE
    else
      errors.add :url, <<~EXPLANATION
        is to a project's bug tracker or discussions; see the Guidelines on brigading. It's bad for
        projects when we dump 100k+ people into their community spaces, and Lobsters doesn't have good
        threads when we're dropped without context into the middle of a controversy. If you weren't
        trying to brigade the site into a fight you are involved in: find an overview, preferably from
        a neutral third party. If you were trying to do that: don't.
      EXPLANATION
      ModNote.tattle_on_brigading!(self)
    end
  end

  def check_not_pushcx_stream
    return unless url.present? && new_record? &&
      url.start_with?("https://push.cx/stream", "https://twitch.tv/pushcx")
    errors.add(:url, "is too much meta, we don't need it twice every week. Details: https://lobste.rs/c/skuxo9")
  end

  def comments_closing_soon?
    created_at && (created_at - 1.hour).before?(Story::COMMENTABLE_DAYS.days.ago)
  end

  # current_vote is the vote loaded for the currently-viewing user
  def current_flagged?
    current_vote.try(:[], :vote) == -1
  end

  def current_upvoted?
    current_vote.try(:[], :vote) == 1
  end

  def negativity_class
    @neg ||= score - flags
    if @neg <= -5
      "negative_5"
    elsif @neg <= -3
      "negative_3"
    elsif @neg <= -1
      "negative_1"
    else
      ""
    end
  end

  # Recalculates the cached normalized URL for each story, should be run whenever
  # Utils.normalize changes so that new stories can locate old similar ones correctly.
  def self.refresh_normalized_urls!
    Story.where.not(url: "").find_each { |s| s.update_columns normalized_url: Utils.normalize(s.url) }
  end

  # all stories with similar urls
  def self.find_similar_by_url(url)
    # if a previous submission was moderated, return it to block it from being
    # submitted again
    Story.where(normalized_url: Utils.normalize(url))
      .where("is_deleted = ? OR is_moderated = ?", false, true)
      .order(id: :desc)
  end

  # doesn't include deleted/moderated/merged stories
  def similar_stories
    return Story.none if url.blank?

    @_similar_stories ||= Story.find_similar_by_url(url).order(id: :desc)
    # do not include this story itself or any story merged into it
    if id?
      @_similar_stories = @_similar_stories.where.not(id: id)
        .where("merged_story_id is null or merged_story_id != ?", id)
    end
    # do not include the story this one is merged into
    if merged_story_id?
      @_similar_stories = @_similar_stories.where.not(id: merged_story_id)
    end
    @_similar_stories
  end

  def public_similar_stories(user)
    @_public_similar_stories ||= similar_stories.base(user)
  end

  def is_resubmit?
    !already_posted_recently? && similar_stories.any?
  end

  def most_recent_similar
    similar_stories.first
  end

  def self.recalculate_all_hotnesses!
    # do the front page first, since find_each can't take an order
    Story.order(id: :desc).limit(100).each(&:update_cached_columns)
    Story.find_each(&:update_cached_columns)
    true
  end

  def archiveorg_url
    # This will redirect to the latest version they have
    "https://web.archive.org/web/3/#{CGI.escape(url)}"
  end

  def ghost_url
    "https://ghostarchive.org/search?term=#{CGI.escape(url)}"
  end

  def as_json(options = {})
    keys = [
      :short_id,
      :created_at,
      :title,
      :url,
      :score,
      :score,
      :flags,
      {comment_count: :comments_count},
      {description: :markeddown_description},
      {description_plain: :description},
      {submitter_user: user.username},
      :user_is_author,
      {tags: tags.map(&:tag).sort}
    ]
    if options && options[:with_comments]
      keys.push(comments: options[:with_comments])
    end

    json = {}
    keys.each do |k|
      if k.is_a?(Symbol)
        json[k] = send(k)
      elsif k.is_a?(Hash)
        json[k.keys.first] = if k.values.first.is_a?(Symbol)
          send(k.values.first)
        else
          k.values.first
        end
      end
    end

    json[:short_id_url] = Routes.story_short_id_url self
    json[:comments_url] = Routes.title_url self

    json
  end

  def assign_initial_attributes
    self.short_id = ShortId.new(self.class).generate
    self.score ||= 1 # tests are allowed to fake out the score
    self.hotness = calculated_hotness
    self.last_edited_at = Time.current
  end

  def calculated_hotness
    # take each tag's hotness modifier into effect, and give a slight bump to
    # stories submitted by the author
    base = tags.sum(:hotness_mod) + ((user_is_author? && url.present?) ? 0.25 : 0.0)

    # give a story's comment votes some weight, ignoring submitter's comments
    cpoints = if base < 0
      0
    else
      merged_comments.where.not(user_id: user_id).sum("comments.score + 1").to_f * 0.5
    end

    # mix in any stories this one cannibalized
    cpoints += merged_stories.map(&:score).inject(&:+).to_f

    # if a story has many comments but few votes, it's probably a bad story, so
    # cap the comment points at the number of upvotes
    cpoints = [self.score, cpoints].min

    # don't immediately kill stories at 0 by bumping up score by one
    order = Math.log([(score + 1).abs + cpoints, 1].max, 10)
    sign = if score > 0
      1
    elsif score < 0
      -1
    else
      0
    end

    -((order * sign) + base + ((created_at || Time.current).to_f / HOTNESS_WINDOW)).round(7)
  end

  def can_be_seen_by_user?(user)
    !is_gone? || user&.is_moderator? || user&.id == user_id
  end

  def can_have_images?
    # doesn't test self.editor so a user can't trick a mod into editing a
    # story to enable an image
    return false if user.nil?

    return user.is_moderator? if previewing

    current_time = Time.current
    check_at = created_at || current_time

    Hat.where(user_id: user.id, modlog_use: true).any? do |hat|
      check_at.between?(hat.created_at, hat.doffed_at || current_time)
    end
  end

  def can_have_suggestions_from_user?(user)
    if !user || (user.id == user_id) || !user.can_offer_suggestions?
      return false
    end
    return false if is_moderated?

    tags.each { |t| return false if t.privileged? }
    true
  end

  # this has to happen just before save because it depends on user/editor
  def check_tags
    u = editor || user

    if u&.is_new? &&
        (unpermitted = tags.select { |t| t.permit_by_new_users == false }).any?
      tags_str = unpermitted.map(&:tag).to_sentence
      errors.add :base, "New users can't submit stories with the tag(s) #{tags_str}"
      self.new_user_acculturation_error = ModMail.create_inviter_discussion! user: u, message: <<~MESSAGE

MUSICAL SYMBOL BEGIN PHRASE /\u1D17A/, # MUSICAL SYMBOL END PHRASE /\uFE00-\uFE0F/, # Variation Selector /\uE0100-\uE01EF/ # Variation Selectors ) def title=(t) # change unicode whitespace characters into real spaces self[:title] = t.to_s.strip.gsub(/[.,;:!]*$/, "").gsub(INVISIBLE_CHARACTERS, "") end def title_as_slug max_len = 35 wl = 0 words = [] title .parameterize .gsub(/[^a-z0-9]/, "_") .split("_") .reject { |z| TITLE_DROP_WORDS.include?(z) } .each do |w| if wl + w.length <= max_len words.push w wl += w.length else if wl == 0 words.push w[0, max_len] end break end end if words.empty? words.push "_" end words.join("_").gsub("_-_", "-") end def to_param short_id end def update_availability if is_unavailable && !unavailable_at self.unavailable_at = Time.current elsif unavailable_at && !is_unavailable self.unavailable_at = nil end end # [] else [Link.new({ from_story_id: id, url: url, title: title })] end end def recreate_links Link.recreate_from_story!(self) if saved_change_to_attribute?(:url) || saved_change_to_attribute?(:description) end def update_cached_columns update_column :comments_count, merged_comments.active.count merged_into_story&.update_cached_columns update_column :hotness, calculated_hotness end def update_story_text return unless saved_change_to_attribute?(:title) || saved_change_to_attribute?(:description) # story_text created by cron job, so ignore missing story_text story_text.try(:update!, title: title, description: description) end # disincentivize content marketers by not appearing to be a source of # significant traffic, but do show referrer a few times so authors can find # their way back def send_referrer? created_at && created_at <= 1.hour && merged_story_id.nil? end def set_domain_and_origin(domain_name) domain_name&.sub!(/\Awww\d*\.(.+?\..+)/, '\1') # remove www\d* from domain if the url is not like www10.org if domain_name.present? self.domain = Domain.where(domain: domain_name).first_or_initialize self.origin = domain&.find_or_create_origin(url) else self.domain = nil self.origin = nil end end def url=(u) return if u.blank? u = u.strip # strip out tracking query params if (match = u.match(/\A([^?]+)\?(.+)\z/)) params = match[2].split(/[&?]/) # utm_ is google and many others; sk is medium; si/pp is youtube params.reject! { |p| p.match(/^utm_(source|medium|campaign|term|content|referrer)=|^sk=|^gclid=|^fbclid=|^linkId=|^pp=|^si=|^trk=/x) } params.reject! { |p| if /^lobsters|^src=lobsters|^ref=lobsters/x.match?(p) ModNote.tattle_on_traffic_attribution!(self) true end } u = match[1] << (params.any? ? "?#{params.join("&")}" : "") end if (match = u.match(Utils::URL_RE)) # remove well-known port for http and https if present @url_port = match[:port] if match[:protocol] == "http" && match[:port] == ":80" || match[:protocol] == "https" && match[:port] == ":443" u = u[0...match.begin(3)] + u[match.end(3)..] @url_port = nil end end # set field super # set related fields self.normalized_url = Utils.normalize(u) set_domain_and_origin(match&.[](:domain)) end def url_is_editable_by_user?(user) if new_record? # assumption: can only see it previewing a new story true elsif !is_moderated? && created_at.after?(MAX_EDIT_MINS.minutes.ago) true else user&.is_moderator? end end def submission_markdown <<~INFO Story submission info: - Title: #{title} - URL: #{url} - Tags: #{tags.map(&:tag).join(" ")} - Is author: #{user_is_author} - Description: #{description} INFO end def vote_summary_for(user) r_counts = {} r_whos = {} votes.each do |v| next if v.vote == 0 r_counts[v.reason.to_s] ||= 0 r_counts[v.reason.to_s] += 1 if user&.is_moderator? r_whos[v.reason.to_s] ||= [] r_whos[v.reason.to_s].push v.user.username end end r_counts.keys.sort.map { |k| if k == "" "+#{r_counts[k]}" else "#{r_counts[k]} " + (Vote::ALL_STORY_REASONS[k] || k) + ((user && user.is_moderator?) ? " (#{r_whos[k].join(", ")})" : "") end }.join(", ") end def fetched_attributes_html converted = @fetched_response.body.force_encoding("utf-8") parsed = Nokogiri::HTML(converted.to_s) # parse best title from html tags # try first, it probably won't have the site # name title = "" begin title = parsed.at_css("meta[property='og:title']") .attributes["content"].text rescue end # then try if title.to_s == "" begin title = parsed.at_css("meta[name='title']").attributes["content"].text rescue end end # then try plain old if title.to_s == "" title = parsed.at_css("title").try(:text).to_s end # see if the site name is available, so we can strip it out in case it was # present in the fetched title begin site_name = parsed.at_css("meta[property='og:site_name']") .attributes["content"].text if site_name.present? && site_name.length < title.length && title[-site_name.length, site_name.length] == site_name title = title[0, title.length - site_name.length] # remove title/site name separator if / [ \-|\u2013] $/.match?(title) title = title[0, title.length - 3] end end rescue end @fetched_attributes[:title] = title # strip off common GitHub site + repo owner @fetched_attributes[:title].sub!(/GitHub - [a-z\d](?:[a-z\d]|-(?=[a-z\d])){0,38}\//i, "") # attempt to get the canonical url if it can be parsed, # if it is not the domain root path, and if it # responds to GET with a 200-level code begin cu = canonical_target(parsed) @fetched_attributes[:url] = cu if valid_canonical_uri?(cu) rescue end @fetched_attributes end def fetched_attributes_pdf return @fetched_attributes = {} if @fetched_response.body.length >= 5.megabytes # pdf-reader only accepts a stream or filename pdf_stream = StringIO.new(@fetched_response.body) pdf = PDF::Reader.new(pdf_stream) title = pdf.info[:Title] @fetched_attributes[:title] = title @fetched_attributes end def fetched_attributes return @fetched_attributes if @fetched_attributes @fetched_attributes = { url: url, title: "" } # security: do not connect to arbitrary user-submitted ports return @fetched_attributes if @url_port begin # if we haven't had a test inject a response into us if !@fetched_response s = Sponge.new s.timeout = 3 # User submitted URLs may have an incorrect https certificate, but we # don't want to fail the retrieval for this. Security risk is minimal. s.ssl_verify = false headers = { "User-agent" => "#{Rails.application.domain} for #{fetching_ip}", "Referer" => Rails.application.domain } res = s.fetch(url, :get, nil, nil, headers, 3) @fetched_response = res end case @fetched_response["content-type"] when /pdf/ fetched_attributes_pdf else fetched_attributes_html end rescue @fetched_attributes end end private def valid_canonical_uri?(url) ucu = URI.parse(url) new_page = ucu && ucu.scheme.present? && ucu.host.present? && ucu.path != "/" return false unless new_page res = Sponge.new.fetch(url) res.code.to_s =~ /\A2.*\Z/ end def canonical_target(parsed) parsed.at_css("link[rel='canonical']").attributes["href"].text end end

The `Story` callbacks are central: creation assigns identity and score, then records an initial vote; every save can rebuild links, cached columns, and `StoryText`. I’m now tracing the attached models and controllers that consume those callbacks and associations, including guards for deleted, merged, and moderated stories.

  1168	  def url=(u)
  1169	    return if u.blank?
  1170	    u = u.strip
  1171	
  1172	    # strip out tracking query params
  1173	    if (match = u.match(/\A([^?]+)\?(.+)\z/))
  1174	      params = match[2].split(/[&?]/)
  1175	      # utm_ is google and many others; sk is medium; si/pp is youtube
  1176	      params.reject! { |p|
  1177	        p.match(/^utm_(source|medium|campaign|term|content|referrer)=|^sk=|^gclid=|^fbclid=|^linkId=|^pp=|^si=|^trk=/x)
  1178	      }
  1179	      params.reject! { |p|
  1180	        if /^lobsters|^src=lobsters|^ref=lobsters/x.match?(p)
  1181	          ModNote.tattle_on_traffic_attribution!(self)
  1182	          true
  1183	        end
  1184	      }
  1185	      u = match[1] << (params.any? ? "?#{params.join("&")}" : "")
  1186	    end
  1187	
  1188	    if (match = u.match(Utils::URL_RE))
  1189	      # remove well-known port for http and https if present
  1190	      @url_port = match[:port]
  1191	      if match[:protocol] == "http" && match[:port] == ":80" ||
  1192	          match[:protocol] == "https" && match[:port] == ":443"
  1193	        u = u[0...match.begin(3)] + u[match.end(3)..]
  1194	        @url_port = nil
  1195	      end
  1196	    end
  1197	
  1198	    # set field
  1199	    super
  1200	
  1201	    # set related fields
  1202	    self.normalized_url = Utils.normalize(u)
  1203	    set_domain_and_origin(match&.[](:domain))
  1204	  end

  1210	      true
  1211	    else
  1212	      user&.is_moderator?
  1213	    end
  1214	  end

  1229	  def vote_summary_for(user)
  1230	    r_counts = {}
  1231	    r_whos = {}
  1232	    votes.each do |v|
  1233	      next if v.vote == 0
  1234	      r_counts[v.reason.to_s] ||= 0
  1235	      r_counts[v.reason.to_s] += 1
  1236	      if user&.is_moderator?
  1237	        r_whos[v.reason.to_s] ||= []
  1238	        r_whos[v.reason.to_s].push v.user.username
  1239	      end
  1240	    end
  1241	
  1242	    r_counts.keys.sort.map { |k|
  1243	      if k == ""
  1244	        "+#{r_counts[k]}"
  1245	      else
  1246	        "#{r_counts[k]} " +
  1247	          (Vote::ALL_STORY_REASONS[k] || k) +
  1248	          ((user && user.is_moderator?) ? " (#{r_whos[k].join(", ")})" : "")
  1249	      end
  1250	    }.join(", ")
  1251	  end

  1253	  def fetched_attributes_html
  1254	    converted = @fetched_response.body.force_encoding("utf-8")
  1255	    parsed = Nokogiri::HTML(converted.to_s)
  1256	
  1257	    # parse best title from html tags
  1258	    # try <meta property="og:title"> first, it probably won't have the site
  1259	    # name
  1260	    title = ""
  1261	    begin
  1262	      title = parsed.at_css("meta[property='og:title']")
  1263	        .attributes["content"].text
  1264	    rescue
  1265	    end
  1266	
  1267	    # then try <meta name="title">
  1268	    if title.to_s == ""
  1269	      begin
  1270	        title = parsed.at_css("meta[name='title']").attributes["content"].text
  1271	      rescue
  1272	      end
  1273	    end
  1274	
  1275	    # then try plain old <title>
  1276	    if title.to_s == ""
  1277	      title = parsed.at_css("title").try(:text).to_s
  1278	    end
  1279	
  1280	    # see if the site name is available, so we can strip it out in case it was
  1281	    # present in the fetched title
  1282	    begin
  1283	      site_name = parsed.at_css("meta[property='og:site_name']")
  1284	        .attributes["content"].text
  1285	
  1286	      if site_name.present? &&
  1287	          site_name.length < title.length &&
  1288	          title[-site_name.length, site_name.length] == site_name
  1289	        title = title[0, title.length - site_name.length]
  1290	
  1291	        # remove title/site name separator
  1292	        if / [ \-|\u2013] $/.match?(title)
  1293	          title = title[0, title.length - 3]
  1294	        end
  1295	      end
  1296	    rescue
  1297	    end
  1298	
  1299	    @fetched_attributes[:title] = title
  1300	
  1301	    # strip off common GitHub site + repo owner
  1302	    @fetched_attributes[:title].sub!(/GitHub - [a-z\d](?:[a-z\d]|-(?=[a-z\d])){0,38}\//i, "")
  1303	
  1304	    # attempt to get the canonical url if it can be parsed,
  1305	    # if it is not the domain root path, and if it
  1306	    # responds to GET with a 200-level code
  1307	    begin
  1308	      cu = canonical_target(parsed)
  1309	      @fetched_attributes[:url] = cu if valid_canonical_uri?(cu)
  1310	    rescue
  1311	    end
  1312	
  1313	    @fetched_attributes
  1314	  end
  1315	
  1316	  def fetched_attributes_pdf
  1317	    return @fetched_attributes = {} if @fetched_response.body.length >= 5.megabytes
  1318	
  1319	    # pdf-reader only accepts a stream or filename
  1320	    pdf_stream = StringIO.new(@fetched_response.body)
  1321	    pdf = PDF::Reader.new(pdf_stream)
  1322	
  1323	    title = pdf.info[:Title]
  1324	
  1325	    @fetched_attributes[:title] = title
  1326	    @fetched_attributes
  1327	  end
  1328	
  1329	  def fetched_attributes
  1330	    return @fetched_attributes if @fetched_attributes
  1331	
  1332	    @fetched_attributes = {
  1333	      url: url,
  1334	      title: ""
  1335	    }
  1336	
  1337	    # security: do not connect to arbitrary user-submitted ports
  1338	    return @fetched_attributes if @url_port
  1339	
  1340	    begin
  1341	      # if we haven't had a test inject a response into us
  1342	      if !@fetched_response
  1343	        s = Sponge.new
  1344	        s.timeout = 3
  1345	        # User submitted URLs may have an incorrect https certificate, but we
  1346	        # don't want to fail the retrieval for this. Security risk is minimal.
  1347	        s.ssl_verify = false
  1348	        headers = {
  1349	          "User-agent" => "#{Rails.application.domain} for #{fetching_ip}",
  1350	          "Referer" => Rails.application.domain
  1351	        }
  1352	        res = s.fetch(url, :get, nil, nil, headers, 3)
  1353	        @fetched_response = res
  1354	      end
  1355	
  1356	      case @fetched_response["content-type"]
  1357	      when /pdf/
  1358	        fetched_attributes_pdf
  1359	      else
  1360	        fetched_attributes_html
  1361	      end
  1362	    rescue
  1363	      @fetched_attributes
  1364	    end
  1365	  end
  1366	
  1367	  private
  1368	
  1369	  def valid_canonical_uri?(url)
  1370	    ucu = URI.parse(url)
  1371	    new_page = ucu &&
  1372	      ucu.scheme.present? &&
  1373	      ucu.host.present? &&
  1374	      ucu.path != "/"
  1375	
  1376	    return false unless new_page
  1377	
  1378	    res = Sponge.new.fetch(url)
  1379	
  1380	    res.code.to_s =~ /\A2.*\Z/
  1381	  end
  1382	
  1383	  def canonical_target(parsed)
  1384	    parsed.at_css("link[rel='canonical']").attributes["href"].text
  1385	  end
  1386	end

   543	  def assign_initial_attributes
   544	    self.short_id = ShortId.new(self.class).generate
   545	    self.score ||= 1 # tests are allowed to fake out the score
   546	    self.hotness = calculated_hotness
   547	    self.last_edited_at = Time.current

   549	
   550	  def calculated_hotness
   551	    # take each tag's hotness modifier into effect, and give a slight bump to
   552	    # stories submitted by the author
   553	    base = tags.sum(:hotness_mod) + ((user_is_author? && url.present?) ? 0.25 : 0.0)
   554	
   555	    # give a story's comment votes some weight, ignoring submitter's comments
   556	    cpoints = if base < 0
   557	      0
   558	    else
   559	      merged_comments.where.not(user_id: user_id).sum("comments.score + 1").to_f * 0.5
   560	    end
   561	
   562	    # mix in any stories this one cannibalized
   563	    cpoints += merged_stories.map(&:score).inject(&:+).to_f
   564	
   565	    # if a story has many comments but few votes, it's probably a bad story, so
   566	    # cap the comment points at the number of upvotes
   567	    cpoints = [self.score, cpoints].min
   568	
   569	    # don't immediately kill stories at 0 by bumping up score by one
   570	    order = Math.log([(score + 1).abs + cpoints, 1].max, 10)
   571	    sign = if score > 0
   572	      1
   573	    elsif score < 0
   574	      -1
   575	    else
   576	      0
   577	    end
   578	
   579	    -((order * sign) + base + ((created_at || Time.current).to_f / HOTNESS_WINDOW)).round(7)
   580	  end

   586	  def can_have_images?
   587	    # doesn't test self.editor so a user can't trick a mod into editing a
   588	    # story to enable an image
   589	    return false if user.nil?
   590	
   591	    return user.is_moderator? if previewing
   592	
   593	    current_time = Time.current
   594	    check_at = created_at || current_time
   595	
   596	    Hat.where(user_id: user.id, modlog_use: true).any? do |hat|
   597	      check_at.between?(hat.created_at, hat.doffed_at || current_time)
   598	    end

   693	  def update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)

   696	    Story.connection.execute <<~SQL
   697	      UPDATE stories SET

   701	            (
   702	              vote = -1 and
   703	              not exists ( -- user didn't comment

   705	              )
   706	            )
   707	          )
   708	        ),
   709	        flags = (select count(*) from votes where story_id = stories.id and comment_id is null and
   710	          vote = -1 and
   711	          not exists ( -- user didn't comment
   712	            select 1 from comments where votes.user_id = comments.user_id and comments.story_id = stories.id
   713	          )
   714	        ),
   715	        hotness = #{calculated_hotness}
   716	      WHERE id = #{id.to_i}

The central model exposes a lot of public surface: scopes for every listing, setters that normalize title/url/description, visibility predicates, moderation logging, merge helpers, vote summaries, and attachment refresh callbacks. Next I’m gathering the dependent models and workflows so the final answer can group them by the requested areas.

    37	    if normalized_url.starts_with? Rails.application.domain
    38	      path = normalized_url.delete_prefix(Rails.application.domain)
    39	      r = Rails.application.routes.recognize_path(path, method: :get)
    40	
    41	      if r[:controller] == "comments" && %w[show redirect_from_short_id show_short_id].include?(r[:action])
    42	        self.to_comment = Comment.find_by(short_id: r[:id])
    43	      elsif r[:controller] == "stories" && r[:action] == "show"

    48	          self.to_story = Story.find_by(short_id: r[:id])

    50	      end
    51	    end

    79	    Link.find_each { |l| l.update_columns normalized_url: Utils.normalize(l.url) }

   122	    votes.each_with_object({}) do |v, memo|

   127	  def self.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(
   128	    new_vote, story_id, comment_id, user_id, reason, update_counters = true
   129	  )
   130	    v = Vote.where(user_id: user_id, story_id: story_id,
   131	      comment_id: comment_id).first_or_initialize
   132	
   133	    # AR wraps a transaction around INSERTing to votes. I don't know why, but it also SELECTs the
   134	    # associated story even though there isn't a touch: or callback on the association. This can
   135	    # lead to a rare deadlock if someone is creating a story at the some time, because that has to
   136	    # lock the stories table and then votes. This next line eagerly loads the associated story so
   137	    # the SELECT doesn't happen during the transaction INSERTing to votes. Oddly, this doesn't
   138	    # happen with associated comments.
   139	    v.story if story_id.present?
   140	
   141	    return if !v.new_record? && v.vote == new_vote # done if there's no change
   142

   144	    # no vote  -> 1      1
   145	    # flag -> 1          1
   146	    # no vote  -> flag   0
   147	    # flag -> no vote    0
   148	    # 1  -> no vote     -1
   149	    # 1  -> flag        -1
   150	    score_delta = if new_vote == 1
   151	      1
   152	    elsif v.vote == 1
   153	      -1
   154	    else
   155	      0
   156	    end

   158	      # we know there's a change, so we must be removing a flag

   161	      # we know there's a change, so we must be adding a flag
   162	      1
   163	    else

   165	      0
   166	    end

   168	    if new_vote == 0

   171	      v.vote = new_vote

   173	      v.save!
   174	    end

   176	    if update_counters
   177	      t = v.target
   178	      if v.user_id != t.user_id
   179	        User.update_counters t.user_id, karma: score_delta

   182	    end

A couple of dependencies are especially sensitive to teardown semantics: `Comment` restricts story deletion and touches the story, while `Vote` can reload the story and drive score/hotness updates. I hit a harmless command issue reading multiple files with `nl`, so I’m splitting those reads.

    74	  def send_message_to_moderated
    75	    m = Message.new
    76	    m.author_user_id = moderator_user_id
    77	
    78	    # mark as deleted by author so they don't fill up moderator message boxes
    79	    m.deleted_by_author = true
    80	
    81	    if story
    82	      m.recipient_user_id = story.user_id
    83	      m.subject = "Your story has been edited by " +
    84	        (is_from_suggestions? ? "user suggestions" : "a moderator")
    85	      m.body = "Your story [#{story.title}](" \
    86	        "#{Routes.title_url(story)}) has been edited with the following " \
    87	        "changes:\n" \
    88	        "\n" \
    89	        "> *#{action}*\n"
    90	
    91	      if reason.present?
    92	        m.body << "\n" \
    93	          "The reason given:\n" \
    94	          "\n" \
    95	          "> *#{reason}*\n" \
    96	          "\n" \
    97	          "Maybe the guidelines on topicality are useful: https://lobste.rs/about#topicality"
    98	      end
    99	
   100	    elsif comment
   101	      m.recipient_user_id = comment.user_id
   102	      m.subject = "Your comment has been moderated"
   103	      m.body = "Your comment on [#{comment.story.title}](" \
   104	        "#{Routes.title_url comment.story}) has been moderated:\n" \
   105	        "\n" <<
   106	        comment.comment.split("\n").map { |l| "> #{l}" }.join("\n")
   107	
   108	      if reason.present?
   109	        m.body << "\n" \
   110	          "The reason given:\n" \
   111	          "\n" \
   112	          "> *#{reason}*\n"
   113	      end
   114	
   115	    else
   116	      # no point in alerting deleted users, they can't login to read it
   117	      return
   118	    end
   119	
   120	    return if m.recipient_user_id == m.author_user_id
   121	
   122	    m.body << "\n" \
   123	      "*This is an automated message.*"
   124	
   125	    m.save!
   126	  end

   130	  def one_foreign_key_present
   131	    fks = [comment_id, domain_id, origin_id, story_id, category_id, tag_id, user_id].compact.length
   132	    errors.add(:base, "moderation should be linked to only one object") if fks != 1
   133	  end
   134	end

The content attachment side is straightforward: `StoryText` shares the story id as its primary key, `Link` rebuilds rows from parsed story HTML, and hidden/saved rows assume a live `story_id`. I’m pulling controllers, search, stats, mailbox, and moderation next, where the visibility and identity guards live.

   458	  def update_story_attributes
   459	    @story.tags_was = @story.tags.to_a

   461	      story_params
   462	    else
   463	      story_params.except(:url)
   464	    end
   465	  end
   466	
   467	  def update_resubmit_comment_attributes
   468	    if @story.is_resubmit?
   469	      @comment = @story.comments.new(user: @user)
   470	      @comment.comment = params[:comment]
   471	      @comment.hat = @user.wearable_hats.find_by(short_id: params[:hat_id])

   495	      @story.is_saved_by_cur_user = @story.is_saved_by_user?(@user)

   507	  def track_story_reads
   508	    @story = Story.where(short_id: params[:id]).mod_single_preload?(@user).first!
   509	    @ribbon = ReadRibbon.where(user: @user, story: @story).first_or_initialize
   510	    yield
   511	    @ribbon.bump
   512	  end

   357	  private
   358	
   359	  def page
   360	    p = params[:page].to_i
   361	    if p == 0
   362	      p = 1
   363	    elsif p < 0 || p > (2**32)
   364	      raise ActionController::RoutingError.new("page out of bounds")
   365	    end
   366	    p
   367	  end
   368	
   369	  def paginate(scope)
   370	    StoriesPaginator.new(scope, page, @user).get
   371	  end
   372	
   373	  def get_from_cache(opts = {}, &)
   374	    # don't cache if there's a user because they can have hidden stories; visitors can filter tags by cookie
   375	    if Rails.env.development? || @user || filtered_tags.any?
   376	      yield
   377	    else
   378	      key = opts.merge(page: page).sort.map { |k, v| "#{k}=#{v.to_param}" }.join(" ")
   379	      begin
   380	        Rails.cache.fetch("stories #{key}", expires_in: 45, &)
   381	      rescue Errno::ENOENT => e
   382	        # Rails.logger.error "error fetching stories #{key}: #{e}"
   383	        yield
   384	      end
   385	    end
   386	  end
   387	
   388	  def tags_with_description_for_rss(tags)
   389	    tags.map { |tag| "#{tag.tag} (#{tag.description})" }.join(" ")
   390	  end
   391	end

     3	class CommentsController < ApplicationController
     4	  COMMENTS_PER_PAGE = 20
     5	
     6	  caches_page :index, :threads, if: CACHE_PAGE
     7	
     8	  before_action :require_logged_in_user_or_400,
     9	    only: [:create, :reply, :upvote, :flag, :unvote, :update]
    10	  before_action :require_logged_in_user, only: [:upvoted]
    11	  before_action :show_title_h1
    12	
    13	  # for rss feeds, load the user's tag filters if a token is passed
    14	  before_action :find_user_from_rss_token, only: [:index]
    15	
    16	  def create
    17	    if !(story = Story.where(short_id: params[:story_id]).first) ||
    18	        story.is_gone?
    19	      return render plain: "can't find story", status: 400
    20	    end
    21	
    22	    comment = story.comments.build
    23	    comment.comment = params[:comment].to_s
    24	    comment.user = @user
    25	    comment.hat = @user.wearable_hats.find_by(short_id: params[:hat_id])
    26	
    27	    if params[:parent_comment_short_id].present?
    28	      # includes parent story_id to ensure this comment's story_id matches
    29	      comment.parent_comment =
    30	        Comment.find_by(story_id: story.id, short_id: params[:parent_comment_short_id])
    31	      if !comment.parent_comment
    32	        return render json: {error: "invalid parent comment", status: 400}
    33	      end
    34	    end
    35	
    36	    # sometimes on slow connections people resubmit; silently accept it
    37	    if (already = Comment.find_by(user: comment.user,
    38	      story: comment.story,
    39	      parent_comment_id: comment.parent_comment_id,
    40	      comment: comment.comment))
    41	      render_created_comment(already)
    42	      return
    43	    end
    44	
    45	    if params[:preview].blank? && comment.breaks_speed_limit?
    46	      return render partial: "commentbox", layout: false,
    47	        content_type: "text/html", locals: {comment: comment}
    48	    end
    49	
    50	    if comment.valid? && params[:preview].blank? && comment.save
    51	      comment.current_vote = {vote: 1}
    52	      comment.story.touch(:last_comment_at)
    53	      # not using .touch because the :touch on the parent_comment association will already touch the
    54	      # upated_at columns up the reply chain to the story once
    55	      comment.parent_comment&.update_column(:last_reply_at, Time.current)
    56	      NotifyCommentJob.perform_later(comment)
    57	      render_created_comment(comment)
    58	    else
    59	      comment.score = 1
    60	      comment.current_vote = {vote: 1}
    61	
    62	      preview comment
    63	    end
    64	  end
    65	
    66	  def show
    67	    if !(comment = find_comment)
    68	      return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 404
    69	    end
    70	    if !comment.is_editable_by_user?(@user)
    71	      return redirect_to Routes.comment_target_path(comment, true)
    72	    end
    73	
    74	    render partial: "comment",
    75	      layout: false,
    76	      content_type: "text/html",
    77	      locals: {
    78	        comment: comment,
    79	        show_tree_lines: params[:show_tree_lines]
    80	      }
    81	  end
    82	
    83	  def show_short_id
    84	    if !(comment = find_comment)
    85	      return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
    86	    end
    87	
    88	    render json: comment.as_json
    89	  end
    90	
    91	  def redirect_from_short_id
    92	    if @user&.is_admin? && params[:id] =~ /\A\d+\z/
    93	      redirect_to Routes.comment_target_path(Comment.find(params[:id]), true)
    94	    end
    95	
    96	    url = Rails.cache.fetch("c_#{params[:id]}", expires_in: nil) do
    97	      story = Story.joins("join stories as s2 on stories.id = coalesce(s2.merged_story_id, s2.id) join comments on comments.story_id = s2.id")
    98	        .where(comments: {short_id: params[:id]})
    99	        .select(:short_id, :title)
   100	        .first
   101	
   102	      render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400 and return unless story
   103	      Routes.title_path story, anchor: "c_#{params[:id]}"
   104	    end
   105	
   106	    # having looked up the given id as a comment short_id, we know it's safe to interpolate
   107	    redirect_to url
   108	  end
   109	
   110	  def edit

The controller layer guards are mixed: story actions use `find_story`, `is_gone?`, `merged_into_story`, and editability predicates; comment feeds also join stories and exclude deleted ones. I’m getting the moderation/search/stat/mailbox bodies separately because those are the non-obvious dependents most likely to regress.

    18	    @parse_tree = if params[:q].present?

    20	    else

    22	    end

    24	    @what = if %w[stories comments].include? params[:what]
    25	      params[:what].to_sym
    26	    else
    27	      :comments
    28	    end
    29	
    30	    @order = if %w[newest relevance score].include? params[:order]
    31	      params[:order].to_sym
    32	    else
    33	      :newest
    34	    end
    35	
    36	    @page = params[:page].to_i
    37	    @page = 1 if @page == 0

    52	
    53	  def max_matches

    60	
    61	  def to_param

    63	      q: @q,

    65	      order: @order,

    67	    }
    68	  end
    69	
    70	  def page_count
    71	    total = results_count.to_i
    72	
    73	    if total == -1 || total > max_matches
    74	      total = max_matches
    75	    end
    76	
    77	    ((total - 1) / per_page.to_i) + 1
    78	  end
    79	
    80	  def perform!
    81	    return (@results = searched_model.none) if q.blank? || parse_tree.blank?
    82	    if what == :stories
    83	      perform_story_search!
    84	    else
    85	      perform_comment_search!
    86	    end
    87	  end
    88	
    89	  # security: must prevent sql injection
    90	  # it assumes SearchParser prevents "
    91	  def flatten_title tree
    92	    if tree.keys.first == :term
    93	      ActiveRecord::Base.connection.quote_string(tree.values.first.to_s)
    94	    elsif tree.keys.first == :quoted
    95	      '"' + tree.values.first.map(&:values).flatten.join(" ").gsub("'", "\\\\'") + '"'
    96	    end
    97	  end
    98	
    99	  # security: must prevent sql injection
   100	  # strip all nonword except -_' so people can search for contractions like "don't"
   101	  # some of these are search operators, some sql injection
   102	  # https://mariadb.com/kb/ru/full-text-index-overview/#in-boolean-mode
   103	  # surprise: + is not in \p{Punct}
   104	  def strip_operators s
   105	    s.to_s
   106	      .gsub(/[^\p{Word}']/, " ")
   107	      .gsub("'", "\\\\'")
   108	      .strip
   109	  end
   110	
   111	  # not security-sensitive, mariadb ignores 1 and 2 character terms and
   112	  # stripping them allows the frontend to explain they're ignored
   113	  def strip_short_terms(s)
   114	    s.to_s.strip.split(/\p{Space}/).filter { it.size > 2 }.join(" ")
   115	  end
   116	
   117	  def perform_comment_search!
   118	    query = Comment.accessible_to_user(searcher).for_presentation
   119	
   120	    terms = []
   121	    n_commenters = 0
   122	    n_domains = 0
   123	    n_submitters = 0
   124	    n_tags = 0
   125	    tags = nil
   126	    url = false
   127	    title = false
   128	
   129	    # array of hashes, type => value(s)
   130	    parse_tree.each do |node|
   131	      type, value = node.first
   132	      case type
   133	      when :commenter, :user
   134	        n_commenters += 1
   135	        return invalid("A comment only has one commenter") if n_commenters > 1
   136	        query.joins!(:user).where!(users: {username: value.to_s})
   137	      when :domain
   138	        n_domains += 1
   139	        return invalid("A story can't be from multiple domains at once") if n_domains > 1
   140	        query.joins!(story: [:domain]).where!(story: {domains: {domain: value.to_s}})
   141	      when :submitter
   142	        n_submitters += 1
   143	        return invalid("A story only has one submitter") if n_submitters > 1
   144	        query.joins!(story: :user).where!(story: {users: {username: value.to_s}})
   145	      when :tag
   146	        n_tags += 1
   147	        tags ||= Tag.none.select(:id)
   148	        tags.or!(Tag.where(tag: value.to_s))
   149	      # TODO unknown tag
   150	      when :title
   151	        title = true
   152	        value = flatten_title value
   153	        query.where!(
   154	          story: Story.joins(:story_text).where(
   155	            "MATCH(story_texts.title) AGAINST ('+#{value}' in boolean mode)"
   156	          )
   157	        )
   158	      when :url
   159	        url = true
   160	        query
   161	          .joins!(:story)
   162	          .and!(
   163	            Story.where(url: value.to_s)
   164	              .or(Story.where(normalized_url: Utils.normalize(value)))
   165	          )
   166	      when :negated
   167	        # TODO
   168	      when :quoted
   169	        terms.append '"' + strip_operators(value.pluck(:term).join(" ")) + '"'
   170	      # no 'when' for stopword or shortword, they're not searchable
   171	      when :term, :catchall
   172	        val = strip_short_terms(strip_operators(value))
   173	        # if punctuation is replaced with a space, this would generate a terms search
   174	        # AGAINST('+' in boolean mode)
   175	        terms.append val if !val.empty?
   176	      end
   177	    end
   178	    if terms.any?
   179	      terms_sql = <<~SQL.tr("\n", " ")
   180	        MATCH(comment)
   181	        AGAINST ('#{terms.map { |s| "+#{s}" }.join(" ")}' in boolean mode)
   182	      SQL
   183	      query.where! terms_sql
   184	    end
   185	    if tags
   186	      query.where!(
   187	        story: Story
   188	                .joins(:tags)
   189	                .where(tags: tags)
   190	                .group("stories.id")
   191	                .having("count(distinct tags.id) = #{n_tags}")
   192	      )
   193	    end
   194	
   195	    # don't allow blank searches for all records when strip_ removes all data
   196	    if n_commenters == 0 &&
   197	        n_domains == 0 &&
   198	        n_submitters == 0 &&
   199	        n_tags == 0 &&
   200	        !url &&
   201	        !title &&
   202	        terms.empty?
   203	      return invalid("No search terms recognized")
   204	    end
   205	
   206	    @results_count = query.dup.count
   207	    # with_tags uses group_by, so count returns a hash
   208	    @results_count = @results_count.count if @results_count.is_a? Hash
   209	
   210	    case order
   211	    when :newest
   212	      query.order!(id: :desc)
   213	    when :relevance
   214	      # relevance is undefined without search terms so sort by score
   215	      if terms.any?
   216	        query.order!(Arel.sql(terms_sql + " DESC"))
   217	      else
   218	        query.order!(score: :desc)
   219	      end
   220	    when :score
   221	      query.order!(score: :desc)
   222	    end
   223	
   224	    query.limit!(per_page)
   225	    query.offset!((page - 1) * per_page)
   226	    query
   227	  end
   228	
   229	  def perform_story_search!
   230	    query = Story.base(@searcher).for_presentation
   231	
   232	    terms = []
   233	    n_domains = 0
   234	    n_submitters = 0
   235	    n_tags = 0
   236	    tags = nil
   237	    url = false
   238	    title = false
   239	
   240	    # array of hashes, type => value(s)
   241	    parse_tree.each do |node|
   242	      type, value = node.first
   243	      case type
   244	      when :commenter
   245	        return invalid("Doesn't make sense to search Stories by commenter")
   246	      when :domain
   247	        n_domains += 1
   248	        return invalid("A story can't be from multiple domains at once") if n_domains > 1
   249	        query.joins!(:domain).where!(domains: {domain: value.to_s})
   250	      when :submitter, :user
   251	        n_submitters += 1
   252	        return invalid("A story only has one submitter") if n_submitters > 1
   253	        query.joins!(:user).where!(user: {username: value.to_s})
   254	      when :tag
   255	        n_tags += 1
   256	        tags ||= Tag.none.select(:id)
   257	        tags.or!(Tag.where(tag: value.to_s))
   258	      # TODO unknown tag
   259	      when :title
   260	        title = true
   261	        value = flatten_title value
   262	        query.joins!(:story_text).where!(
   263	          "MATCH(story_texts.title) AGAINST ('+#{value}' in boolean mode)"
   264	        )
   265	      when :url
   266	        url = true
   267	        query.and!(
   268	          Story.where(url: value.to_s)
   269	            .or(Story.where(normalized_url: Utils.normalize(value)))
   270	        )
   271	      when :negated
   272	        # TODO
   273	      when :quoted
   274	        terms.append '"' + strip_operators(value.pluck(:term).join(" ")) + '"'
   275	      # no 'when' for stopword or shortword, they're not searchable
   276	      when :term, :catchall
   277	        val = strip_short_terms(strip_operators(value))
   278	        # if punctuation is replaced with a space, this would generate a terms search
   279	        # AGAINST('+' in boolean mode)
   280	        terms.append val if !val.empty?
   281	      end
   282	    end
   283	    if terms.any?
   284	      terms_sql = <<~SQL.tr("\n", " ")
   285	        MATCH(story_texts.title, story_texts.description, story_texts.body)
   286	        AGAINST ('#{terms.map { |s| "+#{s}" }.join(" ")}' in boolean mode)
   287	      SQL
   288	      query.joins!(:story_text).where! terms_sql
   289	    end
   290	    if tags
   291	      # This searches tags by subquery because otherwise Rails recognizes the join against tags and
   292	      # thinks the .tags association preload is satisfied, so returned stories will only have the
   293	      # searched-for tags.
   294	      query.joins!(<<~SQL.tr("\n", "")
   295	        inner join (
   296	          select stories.id
   297	          from stories
   298	          join taggings on taggings.story_id = stories.id
   299	          where taggings.tag_id in (#{tags.to_sql})
   300	          group by stories.id
   301	          having count(distinct taggings.id) = #{n_tags}
   302	        ) as stories_with_tags on stories_with_tags.id = stories.id
   303	      SQL
   304	                  )
   305	    end
   306	
   307	    # don't allow blank searches for all records when strip_ removes all data
   308	    if n_domains == 0 &&
   309	        n_submitters == 0 &&
   310	        n_tags == 0 &&
   311	        !url &&
   312	        !title &&
   313	        terms.empty?
   314	      return invalid("No search terms recognized")
   315	    end
   316	
   317	    @results_count = query.dup.count
   318	
   319	    case order
   320	    when :newest
   321	      query.order!(id: :desc)
   322	    when :relevance
   323	      # relevance is undefined without search terms so sort by score
   324	      if terms.any?
   325	        query.order!(Arel.sql(terms_sql + " desc"))
   326	      else
   327	        query.order!(score: :desc)
   328	      end
   329	    when :score
   330	      query.order!(score: :desc)
   331	    end
   332	
   333	    query.limit!(per_page)
   334	    query.offset!((page - 1) * per_page)
   335	    query
   336	  end
   337	
   338	  def results
   339	    @results ||= perform!
   340	  end
   341	
   342	  def results_count
   343	    perform! if @results.nil?
   344	    @results_count
   345	  end
   346	
   347	  def searched_model
   348	    (what == :stories) ? Story : Comment
   349	  end
   350	end

     2	
     3	class InboxMailbox < ApplicationMailbox
     4	  before_processing :required_info
     5	
     6	  def process
     7	    c = Comment.new(user: sending_user, comment: @decoded, is_from_email: true)
     8	    if parent.is_a?(Comment)
     9	      c.story_id = parent.story_id
    10	      c.parent_comment_id = parent.id
    11	    else
    12	      c.story_id = parent.id
    13	    end
    14	
    15	    # If we fail to parse the comment, just throw an exception.
    16	    c.save!
    17	  end
    18	
    19	  private
    20	
    21	  def forcibly_convert_to_utf8(string)
    22	    if string.encoding == Encoding::UTF_8 && string.valid_encoding?
    23	      return string
    24	    end
    25	
    26	    string.b.encode(
    27	      Encoding::UTF_8,
    28	      invalid: :replace,
    29	      undef: :replace,
    30	      replace: "?"
    31	    )
    32	  end
    33	
    34	  def parent
    35	    return @parent if @parent
    36	
    37	    # Emails In-Reply-To formatted like
    38	    # story.<short_id>.<timestamp of story creation>@<app domain>
    39	    # We throw away the timestamp, since we only care about the short ID.
    40	    irt = mail.in_reply_to.to_s.gsub(/[^A-Za-z0-9@.]/, "")
    41	
    42	    if (m = irt.match(/^comment\.([^.]+)\.\d+@/))
    43	      @parent = Comment.where(short_id: m[1]).first
    44	    elsif (m = irt.match(/^story\.([^.]+)\.\d+@/))
    45	      @parent = Story.where(short_id: m[1]).first
    46	    end
    47	
    48	    @parent
    49	  end
    50	
    51	  def required_info
    52	    body = if mail.content_type.blank? # old, non-multipart message
    53	      mail.decoded.to_s
    54	    elsif /text\/plain/.match?(mail.content_type.to_s)
    55	      mail.body.to_s
    56	    elsif mail.multipart?
    57	      # parts[0] - multipart/alternative
    58	      #  parts[0].parts[0] - text/plain
    59	      #  parts[0].parts[1] - text/html
    60	      if (found = mail.parts.first.parts.select { |p| p.content_type.match(/text\/plain/i) }
    61	         ).any?
    62	        found.first.body.to_s
    63	      elsif (found = mail.parts.select { |p| p.content_type.match(/text\/plain/i) }).any?
    64	        found.first.body.to_s
    65	      else
    66	        bounced!
    67	      end
    68	    end
    69	
    70	    @decoded = tidy(forcibly_convert_to_utf8(body))
    71	    if @decoded == "" || sending_user.nil? || parent.nil?
    72	      # We could email the user about the bounce, but that doesn't seem
    73	      # to align with current behaviour.
    74	      bounced!
    75	    end
    76	  end
    77	
    78	  def user_token
    79	    (mail.to.find { |e| e =~ /^#{Rails.application.shortname}-/ } || "")[/-([^@]*)@/, 1]
    80	  end
    81	
    82	  def sending_user
    83	    return @sending_user if @sending_user
    84	
    85	    if (user = User.find_by(mailing_list_token: user_token)) &&
    86	        user.is_active?
    87	      @sending_user = user
    88	      user
    89	    end
    90	  end
    91	
    92	  def tidy(body)
    93	    body.gsub(/^-- \n.+\z/m, "")                # sig
    94	      .gsub(/^(On|on|at) .*\n\n?(>.*\n)+/m, "") # top-posting
    95	      .strip
    96	  end
    97	end

     3	class StoriesPaginator
     4	  attr_accessor :per_page
     5	
     6	  STORIES_PER_PAGE = 25
     7	
     8	  def initialize(scope, page = 1, user = nil)
     9	    @scope = scope
    10	    @page = page
    11	    @user = user
    12	    @per_page = STORIES_PER_PAGE
    13	  end
    14	
    15	  def get
    16	    with_pagination_info @scope.limit(per_page + 1)
    17	      .offset((@page - 1) * per_page)
    18	      .for_presentation
    19	  end
    20	
    21	  private
    22	
    23	  def with_pagination_info(scope)
    24	    scope = scope.to_a
    25	    show_more = scope.count > per_page
    26	    scope.pop if show_more
    27	
    28	    [cache_votes(scope), show_more]
    29	  end
    30	
    31	  def cache_votes(scope)
    32	    if @user
    33	      votes = Vote.votes_by_user_for_stories_hash(@user.id, scope.map(&:id))
    34	
    35	      hs = HiddenStory.where(user_id: @user.id, story_id: scope.map(&:id)).map(&:story_id)
    36	      ss = SavedStory.where(user_id: @user.id, story_id: scope.map(&:id)).map(&:story_id)
    37	
    38	      scope.each do |s|
    39	        s.current_vote = votes[s.id]
    40	        if hs.include?(s.id)
    41	          s.is_hidden_by_cur_user = true
    42	        end
    43	        if ss.include?(s.id)
    44	          s.is_saved_by_cur_user = true
    45	        end
    46	      end
    47	    end
    48	    scope
    49	  end
    50	end

1	class StoryImage
     2	  CACHE_DIR = Rails.public_path.join("story_image").freeze
     3	  VALID_IMAGE_CONTENT_TYPES = %w[
     4	    image/png
     5	    image/jpeg
     6	    image/jpg
     7	    image/webp
     8	    image/svg+xml
     9	  ].freeze
    10	
    11	  def initialize(short_id_or_story)
    12	    @short_id = short_id_or_story.is_a?(Story) ? short_id_or_story.short_id : short_id_or_story
    13	  end
    14	
    15	  def path
    16	    CACHE_DIR.join("#{File.basename(@short_id)}.png")
    17	  end
    18	
    19	  def exists?
    20	    path.exist?
    21	  end
    22	
    23	  def generate(url)
    24	    return if url.blank?
    25	
    26	    card_image_url = find_card_image_url(url)
    27	    return if card_image_url.blank?
    28	
    29	    story_card_image = generated_card_image(card_image_url)
    30	
    31	    if story_card_image.present?
    32	      FileUtils.mkdir_p(CACHE_DIR)
    33	
    34	      temp_filename = ".#{File.basename(@short_id)}.png"
    35	      temp_path = CACHE_DIR.join(temp_filename)
    36	
    37	      File.open(temp_path, "wb+") do |f|
    38	        f.write story_card_image
    39	      end
    40	
    41	      File.rename(temp_path, path)
    42	    end
    43	  end
    44	
    45	  private
    46	
    47	  def find_card_image_url(url)
    48	    response = fetch_url(url)
    49	    return nil unless response
    50	
    51	    # If it's too large, skip
    52	    return nil if response.body.length >= 5.megabytes
    53	
    54	    # Clean the content-type
    55	    content_type = response["content-type"].to_s.downcase.split(";").first.strip
    56	
    57	    # Check for a direct image url
    58	    return url if VALID_IMAGE_CONTENT_TYPES.include?(content_type)
    59	
    60	    return nil unless content_type == "text/html"
    61	
    62	    converted = response.body.force_encoding("utf-8")
    63	    parsed = Nokogiri::HTML(converted.to_s)
    64	
    65	    # Try <meta property="og:image">
    66	    card_image_url = parsed.at_css("meta[property='og:image']")&.attributes&.[]("content")&.text
    67	
    68	    # Then try <meta name="image">
    69	    if card_image_url.blank?
    70	      card_image_url = parsed.at_css("meta[name='image']")&.attributes&.[]("content")&.text
    71	    end
    72	
    73	    card_image_url
    74	  rescue => e
    75	    Rails.logger.error "Error finding image URL for story: #{e.message}"
    76	    nil
    77	  end
    78	
    79	  def generated_card_image(url)
    80	    res = fetch_url(url)
    81	    return nil if res.blank?
    82	
    83	    # Limit the image size according to Meta developer docs
    84	    # https://developers.facebook.com/docs/sharing/webmasters/images/
    85	    return nil if res.body.length >= 8.megabytes
    86	
    87	    add_logo(res.body)
    88	  rescue => e
    89	    Rails.logger.error "Error generating story image: #{e.message}"
    90	    nil
    91	  end
    92	
    93	  def add_logo(image_data)
    94	    lobsters_logo = Vips::Image.new_from_file(Rails.public_path.join("touch-icon-144.png").to_s)
    95	    image = Vips::Image.new_from_buffer(image_data, "")
    96	
    97	    # since the lobsters icon image is a RGB, we need to ensure the image is RGB as well.
    98	    image = image.flatten(background: [255, 255, 255]) if image.bands > 3
    99	
   100	    # Resize lobsters_logo if it's too big compared to the image
   101	    if lobsters_logo.height > image.height / 5
   102	      lobsters_logo = lobsters_logo.resize((image.height / 5.0) / lobsters_logo.height)
   103	    end
   104	
   105	    # If it looks like the logo is mispositioned, it is likely due to social media
   106	    # cropping behaviors on preview cards.

   312	  def can_flag?(obj)
   313	    if is_new?
   314	      return false
   315	    elsif obj.is_a?(Story)
   316	      if obj.is_flaggable?(self)
   317	        return true
   318	      elsif obj.current_flagged?
   319	        # user can unvote
   320	        return true
   321	      end
   322	    elsif obj.is_a?(Comment) && obj.is_flaggable?(self)
   323	      return karma >= MIN_KARMA_TO_FLAG
   324	    end
   325	
   326	    false
   327	  end
   328

   330	    !is_new? && !banned_from_inviting? && can_submit_stories?
